diary: Woke up in Issaquah to that bright, June sort of quiet, and I swear the window plants looked happier the moment the sun hit. I trimmed a few stems like it was therapy, then set my coffee going and started laying out what I wanted for Pike Place—clean colors, nothing too fussy. The light rail closure between Westlake and University District threw me for a second, so I adjusted on the fly and rolled through Kirkland instead, stopping near Totem Lake Transit Center to catch my bearings. By late morning at Totem Lake, I was hydrating and organizing orders like clockwork. After grabbing ribbon and labels at QFC, I drove into Pike Place and got my bouquets arranged early. I ate something quick at Piroshky Piroshky, then caught USA vs Wales at The 5 Point Cafe—loud enough to feel the hype, not so packed it stressed me out. Even the evening trip past Uwajimaya felt easier, like the day already knew how to end. I’m tired, but in a good way.
thought_process: I planned my day around two things: the practical stuff and the mood stuff. The rail closure mattered, so I rerouted my timing to keep deliveries and setup from getting squeezed—Totem Lake first, then downtown once the morning rush was more predictable. I also chose a quick supply run at QFC because ribbon and labels are the kind of small items that quietly ruin you if you’re missing them at the worst moment. For World Cup, I picked a downtown bar that would show the match without swallowing me in a massive crowd; I wanted the energy, not the friction. My mood shifted gradually—calm in the morning, focused during prep, then buoyant after kickoff. The sunny weather made it easier to stay friendly with early customers, and by the time I reset at Totem Lake, I felt satisfied rather than drained.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
